text: Testing plays a vital role in software development. Two common types are unit tests and integration tests. Unit tests check individual components, like a single function, to ensure they work correctly in isolation. Integration tests verify that different parts of the system work together as expected. A popular approach is test-driven development, or TDD, where you write a test before writing the actual code. This helps clarify the requirements and guides the design. Together, these practices give developers confidence. When you have a strong set of tests, you can make changes or add new features without worrying about breaking existing functionality, knowing the tests will catch any problems early.
